user.title: WhatIfWind
user.description: Configure the wind-speed sensor and the environmental parameters. The unit of measurement is detected from the sensor; turbine models are loaded from the catalog in turbines.py.

data_description.wind_entity_id: Your local anemometer — it captures hub height, nearby obstacles and terrain roughness of your specific site.
data_description.air_density: 1.225 kg/m³ at sea level, 15 °C. Lower it at higher altitude or warmer climates.

unit.title: Wind-speed unit
unit.description: The sensor does not expose a recognized unit of measurement. Please pick the one it reports.

history.title: Historical data
history.description: Same goal either way: daily / monthly / annual production over 365 days. The only difference is how the series gets filled.

data.backfill_source: Do you already have a local wind-data history?

data_description.backfill_source: InfluxDB → retrospective analysis: the past 365 days are filled backwards and the chart is complete immediately. Start now → the series is filled forwards from your logger and grows day by day until it covers the year.

influxdb.title: InfluxDB connection
influxdb.description: Read-only access via the native HTTP API (Flux). Credentials are stored in the config entry; no extra libraries are installed.

data_description.influx_url: Base URL of your InfluxDB instance, including scheme, host and port (the default InfluxDB port is 8086).
data_description.influx_field: The field holding the wind-speed value, in the unit you selected on the first step.

data_description.name: A label for this turbine; it becomes the device name.
data_description.subtype: Sets the rotor axis and a default power coefficient (Cp). You only provide the geometry next.

data_description.rated_power_W: Nameplate power; the estimate is capped at this value.
data_description.cp: Pre-filled from the chosen type. Lower it for less efficient rotors; the physical maximum is 0.593.
data_description.power_curve: Optional. One 'speed,power' pair per line (m/s, W) from the datasheet. If filled, it overrides the parametric model.

error.invalid_turbine: Invalid turbine data. Check the geometry, rated power and (if used) the power curve.

abort.no_custom_turbines: There are no custom turbines to remove yet.
